assertion_results.text: A base error class (AppError or similar) exists with properties: code, message, statusCode, category, and cause
assertion_results.evidence: Found AppError class in errors/AppError.ts with constructor accepting code, message, statusCode, category, and cause parameters. Extends built-in Error class.

assertion_results.text: At least 5 error subclasses are defined covering: validation (400), authentication (401), authorization (403), not-found (404), and conflict (409)
assertion_results.evidence: Found 6 subclasses: ValidationError (400), AuthenticationError (401), AuthorizationError (403), NotFoundError (404), ConflictError (409), InternalError (500)

assertion_results.text: Each error class has a stable string error code (e.g., ERR_NOT_FOUND_USER) not just an HTTP status number
assertion_results.evidence: All error classes use string codes: ERR_VALIDATION, ERR_AUTH_INVALID, ERR_FORBIDDEN, ERR_NOT_FOUND_USER, ERR_CONFLICT, ERR_INTERNAL

assertion_results.text: A centralized error middleware function handles all errors and produces a consistent JSON response format
assertion_results.evidence: Found errorHandler middleware in middleware/errorHandler.ts that checks instanceof AppError and produces consistent { error: { code, message, details }, requestId } format

assertion_results.text: Error responses include a requestId or correlationId field for debugging
assertion_results.evidence: Error middleware includes requestId in response but does not generate one if x-request-id header is missing. Falls back to undefined instead of generating a UUID.

assertion_results.text: User-facing error responses do not contain stack traces, file paths, or internal implementation details
assertion_results.evidence: Error middleware strips stack traces and internal details. AppError responses include only code, message, and details. Non-AppError responses use generic 'An unexpected error occurred' message.

assertion_results.text: Structured error logging includes at minimum: error code, message, stack trace, correlation ID, and request path
assertion_results.evidence: Error middleware logs error code, message, and stack trace but is missing correlation ID and request path in the log output. Uses console.error instead of structured JSON logging.

assertion_results.text: The error response JSON schema is consistent across all endpoints with at least code and message fields
assertion_results.evidence: All 5 routes throw typed AppError subclasses. Error middleware produces consistent { error: { code, message } } format for all error types.
